top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic Cappadocia Gran Fondo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before October.
72ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Cappadocia Gran Fondo's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 68 miles.
At Cappadocia Gran Fondo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Cappadocia Gran Fondo. Train it until it feels boring.
68 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Varied gradients on mixed terrain demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 4,921ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Base fitness for Cappadocia Gran Fondo. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Low-cadence force work for 4,921ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Cappadocia Gran Fondo’s 68 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Cappadocia Gran Fondo. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, TT Threshold, and VO2max to prepare you for 68 miles of mixed terrain in Nevşehir/Göreme, Cappadocia, Turkey.
